title: Leo arrives—a little early, a lot loved
body: Leo John Paul Fraser was born at 1:46pm Georgetown time, premature and brave, into the arms of Luke & Dana. The whole family, from the Caymans to New Zealand, started cheering (and sobbing) at once.

title: Settling in & a few firsts
body: Leo is doing OK and keeping the nurses busy—a few poos already! “Clever boy,” said the family. Luke says he looks just like his dad.

title: Breathing easier & his very first meal
body: Leo is breathing much better today and had his very first feed—1ml of colostrum. Doesn't sound like much, but for a little guy his size that's basically a Big Mac! Greedy guts. 🍔

title: Into the incubator—and first cuddles
body: A big day: Leo graduated to the incubator, his breathing support is being slowly reduced, and he's now feeding every 2 hours in bigger amounts. Best of all—Luke & Dana got their first cuddles with him today.

title: Going from strength to strength
body: Doing well again today and eating more and more—like the little Fraser legend that he is. Tough for Mum & Dad to leave him each night, but he's in good hands.

title: Off breathing support, all on his own
body: Leo is now off breathing support completely and taking more milk with every feed. He's got a touch of jaundice, so he's chilling under the lights to even out his tan—getting that Cayman glow ready. ☀️

title: Making his mark on the world
body: Leo has gone from 'done some poos' to projectile poos clean out the incubator door. A young man of pace and dexterity—had the whole family in stitches. 💩

title: Graduated to a cot!
body: Leo's moving up in the world—strong enough to leave the incubator and graduate to his own big-boy cot. Such a step forward for our little fighter. Dana scooped him straight up for a cuddle. 🎓

title: “We made a cute baby, if I do say so myself”
body: Proud dad Luke, beaming over his boy in a little red beanie—“we made a cute baby, if I do say so myself.” Dad's biased (a lawyer, after all)—but even so, we couldn't agree more. ❤️
